_meta.description: Science/tech progression for a Folktails survival bootstrap. Costs verified from the official wiki (timberborn.wiki.gg) individual building pages where possible. Numbers marked with _uncertain reflect either conflicting sources or version-sensitive balance values that should be re-verified in-game.
notes: Science is spent to permanently unlock a building/upgrade for the whole colony; it is not consumed per-placement.

A separate, larger science cost also unlocks the 'bot workplace' variant of many workplaces (e.g. Forester +500) so bots can operate them. Those bot-unlock costs are recorded under unlocks[].bot_unlock_cost where known.
Folktails Lodges both shelter and breed beavers (no separate breeding pod needed).

science_production._science_per_day_note: Nominal wiki figure is 24/day (24h); realistic output is ~16/day because a worker only works ~16 game-hours. Rate is 1 SP per worked hour.

science_production.unlock_note: Folktails-exclusive high-throughput science building; backbone of mid/late science.

_science_production_note: Bot factions/other science tools (Numbercruncher 1500, Bot Part Factory 500, Bot Assembler 750) exist but Numbercruncher is Iron Teeth; Inventor + Observatory are the Folktails science path.

unlocks.name: Path / Stairs
unlocks.note: Free and instant, no materials.

unlocks.name: Lumberjack Flag
unlocks.note: Fells trees inside a designated cutting area. Free to build.

unlocks.name: Gatherer Flag
unlocks.note: Harvests berries/food from bushes and trees in range. Free to build.

unlocks.name: Water Pump
unlocks.note: Pumps drinking water up to 2 blocks deep.

unlocks.name: Lodge
unlocks.note: Basic housing + breeding.

unlocks.name: Lumber Mill
unlocks.note: wiki.gg lists 'Available at start'; some guides list ~250 SP. Re-verify in-game.

unlocks.name: Water Wheel
unlocks.note: Needs a double-width block of flowing water. Some guides list 50 SP; wiki.gg lists start.

unlocks.name: Power Wheel
unlocks.note: Beaver-operated backup power, works during droughts.

unlocks.name: Farmhouse
unlocks.note: Plants/harvests crops. Start with kohlrabi (edible raw).

unlocks.name: Dam
unlocks.note: Blocks water, spillway at top. Log-heavy vs levees.

unlocks.name: Large Pile
unlocks.note: Open storage for logs/raw goods.

unlocks.name: Forester
unlocks.note: Plants faction trees/bushes; auto-replants after lumberjacks. Sustainable wood.

unlocks.name: Mini Lodge
unlocks.note: Compact single-beaver housing.

unlocks.name: Levee
unlocks.note: Blocks water completely; stackable waterproof walls for reservoirs.

unlocks.name: Wind Turbine (Windmill)
unlocks.note: Drought-independent power (keeps running when the river stops flowing).

unlocks.name: Floodgate
unlocks.note: Adjustable-height water barrier (0-1 in 0.05 steps) for reservoir control.

unlocks.name: Double Lodge
unlocks.note: Higher-density housing + breeding.

unlocks.name: Triple Lodge
unlocks.note: Dense housing.

unlocks.name: Gravity Battery
unlocks.note: Stores power to run pumps/mills through droughts.

unlocks.name: Bot Part Factory
unlocks.note: First step toward bot workforce.

unlocks.name: Bot Assembler
unlocks.note: Builds bots. Some sources cite up to 10000 for the late variant; re-verify.

unlocks.name: Observatory
unlocks.note: Main mid/late science producer; requires a reliable power source.

unlocks.name: Large Water Wheel
unlocks.note: Iron Teeth power option; listed for completeness, not Folktails.

recommended_order.name: Forester
recommended_order.rationale: Replant felled trees so the wood supply is renewable instead of a one-time deforestation; cheapest high-impact unlock.

recommended_order.name: Mini Lodge
recommended_order.rationale: Cheap early housing to grow the population and therefore the worker pool that everything else depends on.

recommended_order.name: Levee
recommended_order.rationale: Waterproof stackable walls to cheaply wall off a drought reservoir without spending logs on Dams.

recommended_order.name: Wind Turbine (Windmill)
recommended_order.rationale: Drought-proof power: water wheels stall when the river dries, so wind keeps pumps and mills running through dry seasons.

recommended_order.name: Floodgate
recommended_order.rationale: Adjustable water control to fill and hold the reservoir at a chosen level; the core survival-water tool.

recommended_order.name: Double Lodge
recommended_order.rationale: Denser housing/breeding once food and water buffers can support a larger colony.

recommended_order.name: Gravity Battery
recommended_order.rationale: Bank surplus power (from wind/water) so critical pumps and mills survive extended droughts and badtides.

recommended_order.name: Observatory
recommended_order.rationale: Once survival infrastructure is stable, the Observatory multiplies science throughput to unlock the rest of the tree quickly.

_recommended_order_note: Survival-first ordering for Folktails. Start-tier buildings (Water Pump, Lumberjack Flag, Lumber Mill, Dam, Water Wheel, Farmhouse, Large Pile, Lodge) need no science and should be placed before spending any SP.
